0.description: The Fourth Pharos chapter house, late evening. Rain streaks the leaded glass. A single gaslight gutters above the briefing table, casting long shadows across old case files stacked in towers that lean like drunk men.
0.read_aloud: Your Lightkeeper's voice is clipped, urgent. 'A body outside Hargrove's Apothecary on Briar Street. Aged beyond recognition -- turned to dust when the coroner tried to move it. OUP has already closed the block. We need eyes on this before they bury it.'

npcs.name: Lightkeeper Maren Ashwick
npcs.dialogue: The corpse was identified by a pocket watch engraved to one Declan Voss. Voss was twenty-six years old. The body looked ninety. I trust I don't need to spell out why this concerns us.
npcs.notes: Ashwick knows more than she says. She investigated Hargrove's Apothecary a decade ago and found nothing. The case haunts her.

event_triggers: A body has been found outside an abandoned drugstore, aged impossibly. Candela Obscura dispatches the circle.

1.description: The chapter house records room. Filing cabinets line the walls, each drawer labeled in a cipher only Candela operatives can read. A pneumatic tube connects to the main archive below.
1.read_aloud: Dusty files on Hargrove's Apothecary reveal it closed in 1892 after the owner vanished. Neighbors reported music from the basement for years afterward -- then silence.
1.investigation_success: A faded incident report from 1908: an OUP officer responded to a noise complaint at Hargrove's and was never seen again. His badge was found on the sidewalk, tarnished black.

loot.name: Hargrove's Case File
loot.description: A thin folder containing a floor plan of the apothecary, a list of former employees, and a photograph of the basement door -- padlocked from the outside.
